items.definition_en: The small intestine is the long, narrow part of the intestine where most digestion occurs.
items.example_en: Food is mostly broken down in the small intestine.

items.definition_en: The large intestine is the wider part of the intestine that absorbs water and forms waste.
items.example_en: Water is absorbed in the large intestine.

items.definition_en: The duodenum is the first part of the small intestine just after the stomach.
items.example_en: The duodenum receives food and acid from the stomach.

items.definition_en: The rectum is the last part of the large intestine before the anus.
items.example_en: Solid waste collects in the rectum before it leaves the body.

items.definition_en: The pituitary gland is a small gland in the brain that controls growth and hormones.
items.example_en: The pituitary gland releases important hormones for the body.

items.definition_en: An adrenal gland is an organ above each kidney that makes adrenaline and other hormones.
items.example_en: The adrenal gland helps your body react to stress.

items.definition_en: The thyroid gland is located in the neck and controls metabolism by producing hormones.
items.example_en: Your energy depends a lot on a healthy thyroid gland.

items.definition_en: The spleen is an organ that helps filter blood and supports the immune system.
items.example_en: The spleen can be damaged in some accidents.

items.definition_en: A bronchus is one of the main air passages to the lungs.
items.example_en: When you breathe in, air travels down each bronchus to the lungs.

items.definition_en: The urethra is the tube that carries urine from the bladder out of the body.
items.example_en: A urinary tract infection can affect the urethra.

items.definition_en: A ureter is a tube that carries urine from a kidney to the bladder.
items.example_en: A kidney stone can block a ureter.

items.definition_en: A coronary artery is a blood vessel that supplies blood to the heart muscle.
items.example_en: A heart attack can block a coronary artery.

items.definition_en: Plasma is the pale yellow fluid part of blood that carries cells and nutrients.
items.example_en: Doctors can separate plasma from blood donations.

items.definition_en: The atrium is one of the upper chambers of the heart where blood enters.
items.example_en: Oxygen-poor blood enters the heart through the right atrium.

items.definition_en: A ventricle is one of the lower chambers of the heart that pumps blood out.
items.example_en: The left ventricle pumps blood to the whole body.

items.definition_en: A bile duct is the tube that carries bile from the liver and gallbladder to the intestine.
items.example_en: A blocked bile duct can cause stomach pain.

items.definition_en: An alveolus is a tiny air sac in the lungs where oxygen enters the blood.
items.example_en: Each alveolus helps you take in oxygen while breathing.

items.definition_en: The medulla oblongata is part of the brain that controls breathing and heartbeat.
items.example_en: The medulla oblongata keeps the heart and lungs working automatically.

items.definition_en: The hypothalamus is a small brain region controlling hunger, thirst, and temperature.
items.example_en: Your hypothalamus helps keep your body temperature stable.

items.definition_en: The pelvis is the group of bones at the base of the spine that supports the body.
items.example_en: The pelvis protects important organs inside your body.
